purpose: Defines the path placeholder convention used throughout Synapse: {tracker_root} = absolute path to Synapse repo, {project_root} = absolute path to target project. Always absolute, always distinct. Specifies the three-tier {project_root} resolution order (--project flag > {tracker_root}/.synapse/project.json > agent CWD), the integration model with arbitrary projects (Synapse can live anywhere; .synapse/ goes inside the target; all Synapse data stays in {tracker_root}), the multi-project support via per-dashboard task.project_root, the portability checklist (zero npm deps for server, no hardcoded paths, etc.), and the timestamp protocol (always live via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`).
gotchas: {tracker_root} and {project_root} are ALWAYS DIFFERENT locations — workers do code work in {project_root} and write progress files to {tracker_root}. Confusing them is the canonical worker failure.
{project_root} resolution order is strict: --project flag overrides everything, then stored config, then CWD. Never assume CWD without checking the higher-priority sources first.

Only the {project_root}/.synapse/ directory is written into the target project — everything else (dashboards/, tasks/, history/, logs.json) stays at {tracker_root}. Add .synapse/ to project's .gitignore.
Timestamps must always be live via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` — guessed/estimated/hardcoded timestamps break the elapsed timer (which derives durations from these values).
Multi-project support: each dashboard's task.project_root identifies its target project; commands like !status/!logs use the agent's assigned dashboard, not a globally-active project.
patterns: Workers receive BOTH {tracker_root} and {project_root} explicitly in their dispatch prompts.
Synapse-anywhere: tools dir, home folder, or anywhere on disk — the only constraint is that paths are absolute.
Per-dashboard project binding: task.project_root in initialization.json identifies which project a swarm targets.
Portability invariants: zero npm deps for server, no hardcoded paths, no project-specific assumptions, server configurable via PORT env var, works offline.
conventions: All paths use {tracker_root}/{project_root} placeholders in docs — never hardcoded actual paths.
